title: lastrea parallelogramma
intro: Lastrea parallelogramma, also known as the Parallel-leaved Lady Fern, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia, characterized by its lance-shaped fronds and distinctive parallel venation. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the Lastrea parallelogramma plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are often used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs and colds, and soothe digestive problems, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies. Herbal preparations of Lastrea parallelogramma can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
